mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand that drives corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening economic output that boosts near-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening cycles, producing negative returns in >50% of cases, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 195.0 while policy rates exceed 4.0%, the resulting real rate compression forces equity repricing, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered observations over a 10-year sample, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic capacity and wage growth that supports corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 3-year rolling windows, or positive return frequency drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength that accelerates earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal yields positive returns in >50% of cases during recovery phases, or predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward validation.
